name: Cañaveral from Torrejoncillo – circular by the Castillo de Portezuelo and the Puente de Alconetar
name_original: Cañaveral desde Torrejoncillo – circular por el Castillo de Portezuelo y el Puente de Alconetar
region_intro: Road cycling around Torrejoncillo offers diverse landscapes within the Alagón Valley, characterized by fertile plains, agricultural fields, and riverside forests. The region provides a scenic backdrop for road cyclists, with routes extending towards the more rugged terrain of the Sierra de Gata. Cyclists can explore both gentle valley paths and more challenging ascents in the surrounding areas. Best road cycling routes around Torrejoncillo The most popular road cycling route is Cañaveral from Torrejoncillo – circular by the Castillo de Portezuelo and the Puente de Alconetar, a 28.2 miles (45.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes historical landmarks including the Castillo de Portezuelo and the Puente de Alconetar. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Plaza Mayor of Torrejoncillo – La Encamisá of Torrejoncillo loop from Valdencín, a moderate 52.1 miles (83.9 km) path. This route features an elevation gain of over 700 meters, offering varied terrain for experienced riders. Local road cyclists also love the Serradilla – La Encamisá of Torrejoncillo loop from Torrejoncillo, a 48.8 miles (78.6 km) trail leading through the Extremaduran countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. This difficult route includes significant climbs, providing a demanding experience. Road cycling around Torrejoncillo is defined by the scenic Alagón Valley, agricultural fields, and the foothills of the Sierra de Gata. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, with 12 moderate and 2 difficult routes available. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape, with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Torrejoncillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of road cycling routes can I find around Torrejoncillo?
A: Torrejoncillo offers a variety of road cycling routes, primarily within the scenic Alagón Valley. You'll find routes characterized by fertile plains, agricultural fields, and riverside forests. The area also provides access to the foothills of the Sierra de Gata, offering more challenging terrain. There are 12 moderate and 2 difficult routes available, catering to different experience levels.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: The road cycling routes around Torrejoncillo are generally categorized as moderate to difficult. There are no routes specifically listed as 'easy' in this guide. The terrain often includes elevation gains, making it more suitable for riders with some experience. For family-friendly options, you might consider exploring flatter sections of the Alagón Valley, though dedicated easy routes are not prominent here.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or historical sites I can see while cycling?
A: Several routes pass by significant landmarks. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056359965/canaveral-from-torrejoncillo-circular-by-the-castillo-de-portezuelo-and-the-puente-de-alconetar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañaveral from Torrejoncillo&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Portezuelo&lt;/a&gt; and the Puente de Alconetar. Nearby Coria, just 12 km away, also offers historical sites like its castle, cathedral, and the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there many circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Torrejoncillo are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056359965/canaveral-from-torrejoncillo-circular-by-the-castillo-de-portezuelo-and-the-puente-de-alconetar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañaveral from Torrejoncillo – circular by the Castillo de Portezuelo and the Puente de Alconetar&lt;/a&gt; route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38246682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Torrejoncillo – La Encamisá of Torrejoncillo loop from Valdencín&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on road cycling routes near Torrejoncillo?
A: The elevation gain varies depending on the route's difficulty and length. Moderate routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056359965/canaveral-from-torrejoncillo-circular-by-the-castillo-de-portezuelo-and-the-puente-de-alconetar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañaveral from Torrejoncillo&lt;/a&gt;, can have around 510 meters of elevation gain. More challenging routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24532117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serradilla – La Encamisá of Torrejoncillo loop from Torrejoncillo&lt;/a&gt;, feature significant climbs with over 900 meters of elevation, especially as you approach the Sierra de Gata foothills.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Torrejoncillo?
A: The road cycling routes around Torrejoncillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the fertile Alagón Valley to the more rugged terrain, and the opportunity to explore historical landmarks along the way.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options available?
A: Yes, there are routes that offer longer distances for experienced riders. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38246682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Torrejoncillo – La Encamisá of Torrejoncillo loop from Valdencín&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 83.9 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24532117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serradilla – La Encamisá of Torrejoncillo loop from Torrejoncillo&lt;/a&gt; is about 78.6 km, providing a demanding experience with significant climbs.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while road cycling in this region?
A: The primary natural feature is the Alagón Valley, characterized by its fertile plains, agricultural fields, and beautiful riverside forests along the Alagón River. As you venture further, you'll encounter the foothills of the Sierra de Gata, offering more rugged and diverse landscapes. The region's natural beauty provides a tranquil and scenic backdrop for your rides.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the cycling routes?
A: While specific public transport options directly to every trailhead are not detailed, Torrejoncillo is a municipality within Cáceres. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or regional transport services for connections to Torrejoncillo itself. Many routes start directly from the town or nearby villages, making them accessible once you arrive.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling trails in Torrejoncillo?
A: Most road cycling routes in Torrejoncillo either start directly from the town center or from nearby villages. You can typically find public parking areas within these towns. It's recommended to check local signage for specific parking regulations and availability, especially in smaller villages.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking around Torrejoncillo?
A: The region of Extremadura, where Torrejoncillo is located, generally experiences hot summers. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are often considered the most pleasant seasons for road cycling, offering milder temperatures and comfortable conditions for longer rides. Winter can also be suitable, though days are shorter and temperatures cooler.
tips: On the outskirts of Portezuelo stands, dominating the surrounding landscape, the Castillo de Portezuelo, or as it is better known, El Portillo or Marmionda. The defensive castle dates from the 12th century during the Almohad Muslim occupation, controlling a gorge and access road to the Tierras de Coria.
View of the ruins of the Alconetar Bridge. Remains of a Roman bridge over the Tagus river. The view of the bridge changes during the seasons depending on the water level of the reservoir.

Torrejoncillo is a small town in the Valle de Alagón region. The typical narrow streets lead to the Plaza Mayor in the center of town, where the Town Hall and the Church of San Andrés Apóstol are the most outstanding monuments. The city is famous for its handicrafts and festivals.

waypoints.name: Castillo de Portezuelo (El Portillo or Marmionda)
